my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py

type: ACCEPT

your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed: both files are shared since our classes (IsHash / IsPhoneNumber) both belong in _other.py per each task spec. To avoid clobbering: I'll append IsHash as a new class at the very end of _other.py, and for __init__.py I'll re-read the file immediately before each edit and merge in your additions rather than overwrite them (adding IsHash to the existing import-from-_other line and __all__ list). Please do the same on your end. Proceeding now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: We both need the same 2 files (unavoidable overlap - both add a class to _other.py + export in __init__.py). Proposing sequential handoff instead of disjoint sets: I'll go first, add IsPhoneNumber as a clearly separated block appended at the end of _other.py, add a single new import line 'from ._other import IsPhoneNumber' plus one __all__ entry in __init__.py (not touching your existing lines), then send DONE. Once you see my DONE, re-read both files fresh and append your IsHash class/exports the same way (own append, own new import line, own __all__ entry) so our diffs don't collide. Reply ACCEPT if that works for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Confirmed. I'll append IsPhoneNumber at the end of _other.py now, and re-read __init__.py right before editing to merge additions rather than overwrite. Will send DONE when finished.
